codeReview.summary: The modal UX wiring is mostly implemented (Wait/Swap Anyway paths, overlay/Escape close, aria, auto-update hooks), but two requirement-level gaps remain: single-router swaps can still be gated by the modal before complete arrives, and a focus trap is not implemented.

issues.description: `singleRouterMode` is only set on the `complete` event, but swap gating uses `areQuotesStillLoading()` in `onSwapClick` (line 6715) before completion. In single-router cases, users can click Swap on the first rendered quote and incorrectly get the confirmation modal, violating the expected behavior that single-router swaps proceed directly with no modal.

The swap confirmation modal does not implement keyboard focus trapping. Event handling covers close button/overlay/Escape, but there is no Tab/Shift+Tab loop within modal controls, so focus can escape the dialog while it is open, which fails the feature’s explicit focus-trap requirement.

`closeSwapConfirmModal()` sets `pendingSwapCard = null` before attempting focus restoration from that same variable, making the return-focus block unreachable and reducing keyboard UX parity with the MEV modal behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared modal guidance omits focus-trap requirements, which makes it easy to miss accessibility parity when implementing new dialogs.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/fullstack-worker/SKILL.md:82 and mission AGENTS.md:30 describe modal pattern as overlay/Escape/body-scroll/aria only; src/server.ts swap modal listeners at 6008-6044 similarly implement no Tab focus trap.

summary: Review failed for commit c18f619: while core modal interactions are implemented, the feature does not fully satisfy requirements due to missing focus-trap behavior and incorrect single-router gating before completion.
